g.data-narration: Welcome to SQL DML operations. We will explore INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements for database manipulation.
DML commands are essential for managing data in relational databases after creating table structures with DDL.
g.text: DML commands manage data in existing tables:

g.data-narration: Real-world databases constantly change: new products added, records updated, and old data removed for maintenance.
g.text: Why DML Matters in Real-World Applications

g.data-narration: INSERT adds new rows to a table. Use column list for explicit mapping or omit for all columns in order.
Modern databases support inserting multiple rows in a single INSERT statement, improving performance significantly.
g.text: INSERT INTO students (id, name, age, cgpa)

g.data-narration: DEFAULT keyword uses table-defined values, while NULL explicitly inserts null. Understanding this distinction is exam-important.
g.text: INSERT with DEFAULT and NULL Keywords

g.data-narration: INSERT with SELECT enables data migration, archiving, and creating backup tables from existing data.
g.text: -- Copy high performers to archive table

g.data-narration: UPDATE modifies existing data. The WHERE clause is critical - without it, ALL rows get modified accidentally.
g.text: Without WHERE, ALL rows are updated:

g.data-narration: UPDATE supports arithmetic expressions, string concatenation, and multiple column modifications in a single statement.
DELETE removes rows based on conditions. Without WHERE, all rows are deleted but table structure remains.
DELETE is DML (row-by-row, can rollback), TRUNCATE is DDL (deallocates, faster, cannot rollback in most databases).
g.text: DELETE vs TRUNCATE - Know the Difference

g.data-narration: Transactions ensure atomicity: either all operations succeed together, or none do. COMMIT makes changes permanent.
Real-world example: fund transfer uses transaction to ensure money is deducted and credited atomically.
Remember key exam points: WHERE clause importance, DELETE vs TRUNCATE difference, and DEFAULT vs NULL usage.
To summarize: INSERT adds data, UPDATE modifies it, DELETE removes it, and transactions ensure data integrity.
g.text: Use transactions (COMMIT/ROLLBACK) to ensure data integrity in multi-step operations
